name: Hypothyroidism
treatment:
Levothyroxine 1.6mcg/kg/day once daily (first-line)
Start low dose (12.5–50mcg/day) in elderly or cardiac patients
Increase every 6–8 weeks based on TSH response
Take empty stomach, 30–60 minutes before food
Avoid calcium/iron within 4 hours
Lifelong therapy in most primary hypothyroidism cases
Monitor TSH every 6–8 weeks until stable, then every 6–12 months
comprehensiveInfo: Common, often related to iodine deficiency. About 85% of affected individuals are women. Untreated hypothyroidism during pregnancy often results in miscarriage.

supportive care monotherapy
oral hydration, adequate calories, rest — 14–84 days

urgent admission
IV fluids carefully
glucose correction

Albendazole 400mg single dose once
Ferrous sulfate daily for 30–90 days
Repeat after 14 days if pinworm suspected or reinfection risk high

Strong Alternative
Mebendazole 100mg BD for 3 days
OR 500mg single dose once

Heavy Worm Burden/Persistent Symptoms
Albendazole 400mg OD for 3 days

Household Pinworm Control
Treat close contacts simultaneously when possible
Wash bedding/clothes, hand hygiene, trim nails

name: Dyspepsia
treatment:

H. pylori therapy (if positive)
Refractory Functional Dyspepsia

comprehensiveInfo: Persistent or recurrent upper abdominal discomfort or pain associated with bloating, early satiety, nausea, or belching, typically related to eating. H pylori negative or dyspepsia after eradication most have functional dyspepsia or atypical GERD.

symptomDetails.epigastric pain: most common symptom, often related to food (worsens or improves after meals).
symptomDetails.postprandial fullness:
Feeling of uncomfortable fullness soon after eating a normal-sized meal.
Patients feel that food remains in the stomach for a long time.
Common in impaired gastric emptying or functional dyspepsia.
symptomDetails.early satiety:
Inability to finish a normal meal because fullness occurs quickly.
Appears within the first few bites or early during eating.
Often associated with gastric hypersensitivity or delayed emptying.
symptomDetails.bloating/flatulence: Sensation of abdominal distension due to gastric inflammation and altered motility.
symptomDetails.nausea:
Persistent queasy feeling in the stomach.
Usually mild to moderate and related to gastric irritation or delayed gastric emptying.
symptomDetails.belching:
Repeated expulsion of swallowed air from the stomach.
Often associated with gastric distension after meals.
symptomDetails.heartburn:
Burning sensation rising from the stomach toward the chest or throat.
Often worsens after lying down or after heavy meals.
symptomDetails.anal itch: Severe itching around the anus, especially at night. Caused mainly by pinworms (Enterobius vermicularis) laying eggs around the anal region.

name: Peptic Ulcer Disease
treatment:
Esomeprazole 40mg BID + 
Bismuth subsalicylate 265mg two tab QID + 
Doxycycline 100mg BID + 
Metronidazole 500mg BID or TID/Tinidazole 500mg BID
For 10–14 days.
comprehensiveInfo: Duodenal ulcer (pain occurs 2–3 hours after meals or at night, relieved by food or antacids). Gastric ulcer (pain occurs shortly after eating, may worsen with food). Most patient have symptomatic periods several weeks with intervals of months to years which are pain-free. Change of typical rhythmic discomfort to constant or radiating pain may reflect ulcer penetration or perforation. Most NSAID-induced ulcers are asymptomatic; perforation or bleeding may be the initial presentation. If pain radiating to back in inter scapular region and not responding to antacid or anti-ulcer drug, suggests penetration of ulcer into the pancreas.

symptomDetails.heartburn: Patients describe a persistent burning sensation in the upper abdomen due to acid irritation of the ulcerated mucosa.

15-45% spontaneously clear the virus. 85% moves >6 months,

symptomDetails.arthritis/joint pain: Migratory Polyarthritis: Inflammatory arthritis affecting large joints sequentially. Pain and swelling move from one joint to another (commonly knees → ankles → elbows → wrists). Each joint involvement usually lasts 1–3 days. Marked tenderness and limitation of movement occur.
symptomDetails.fever: Usually 38–40°C, moderate to high grade. Fever accompanies inflammatory phase and improves with anti-inflammatory therapy.
symptomDetails.elevated ESR: Markers of systemic inflammation. ESR commonly >50 mm/hr, indicating active inflammatory process.
symptomDetails.subcutaneous nodules: Small painless, firm nodules under skin over bony prominences (elbows, knees, spine). Usually appear during severe carditis.
symptomDetails.erythema marginatum: Non-itchy pink rash with ring-shaped margins. Appears mainly on trunk and proximal limbs. Rash is transient and worsens with heat.
symptomDetails.sydenham chorea: Neurological manifestation characterized by involuntary, irregular, purposeless movements of hands, feet, and face. Associated with emotional instability and muscle weakness.

name: Hypogonadism
exclusionFeatures: normal sexual function with preserved fertility
symptoms fully explained by major depression with normal hormones
severe relationship/performance anxiety with normal hormones
normal pubertal progression and no endocrine symptoms in young
treatment:
Male hypogonadism (primary or secondary, without fertility desire):
Testosterone enanthate 100mg IM weekly or 200mg IM every 2 weeks
Duration: lifelong maintenance.

Male hypogonadism (secondary, with fertility desire):
Clomiphene citrate 25mg daily for 3–6 months
OR
hCG 1500–3000 IU SC/IM 2–3 times weekly

Female hypogonadism:
Conjugated equine estrogen 0.625mg orally daily (or estradiol 1–2mg daily) combined with cyclic medroxyprogesterone acetate 5–10mg daily for 12–14 days per month. Duration: until typical menopause age (50 years) or lifelong if ovarian failure.

Ferrous sulfate (65mg elemental iron/tab)
1 tablet OD or every other day
Duration: 90–180 days, continue 60–90 days after Hb normalization

iron sucrose IV

If uncontrolled after 8–12 weeks
Metformin + Gliclazide MR 30mg daily
OR
Metformin + Glimepiride 1mg daily

Metformin + Empagliflozin 10mg daily

Basal insulin (e.g. insulin glargine) 10units nightly, titrate

Adjunct
Calorie control diet
Walking 30–45 min daily
BP management
Statin if indicated
Foot care
Annual eye/kidney review

name: Asthma

symptoms.typicalSymptom: cough worse at night or early morning
speaks in short sentences during attack

pathognomonicSymptoms: cough worse at night or early morning
treatment:
Acute Mild–Moderate Exacerbation
Salbutamol inhaler 100mcg/puff: 4–10 puffs via spacer every 20min for 1 hour
Prednisolone 40mg daily × 5 days (adult)

Acute Severe Exacerbation
Oxygen target saturation 94–98%
Salbutamol nebulization 2.5–5mg every 20 min × 3
Ipratropium nebulization 0.5mg every 20 min × 3 adjunct
Hydrocortisone IV 100mg every 6–8 hr or prednisolone if tolerated
urgent referral if silent chest, cyanosis, exhaustion, confusion

Long-Term Controller (Best Current Standard)
Budesonide inhaler 200–400mcg/day divided doses
or budesonide/formoterol low-dose as maintenance + reliever if available (preferred)

If Uncontrolled
Increase ICS dose
Add LABA only in combination inhaler
Consider montelukast 10mg nightly adjunct
Check adherence/inhaler technique/smoke exposure
exclusionFeatures: continuous progressive dyspnea with no variability and no bronchodilator response

Hydration
Trigger avoidance

name: COPD
treatment:
Stable Mild COPD
Salbutamol inhaler 2 puffs every 6 hours PRN
Ipratropium inhaler 2 puffs every 6–8 hours PRN if needed

Stable Persistent Symptoms
Tiotropium inhaler 18mcg OD (preferred maintenance)
OR
Formoterol inhaler standard dose BID
If still symptomatic: tiotropium + LABA

Frequent Exacerbations/Severe
LABA + ICS inhaler (example budesonide/formoterol) BID
Triple therapy: LAMA + LABA + ICS if recurrent exacerbations

Acute Exacerbation
Salbutamol nebulization 2.5–5mg every 20 min × 3 then PRN
Ipratropium nebulization 0.5mg every 6 hours adjunct
Prednisolone 40mg daily × 5 days

If bacterial exacerbation likely:
Amoxiclav 625mg TID × 5–7 days
OR
Azithromycin 500mg day 1 then 250mg daily × 4 days
OR
Levofloxacin 500mg daily × 5 days (reserve stronger option)

Non-drug
Smoking cessation
Avoid smoke/dust
oxygen if chronic hypoxemia
exclusionFeatures: age below 35 years without exposure history
sudden onset severe dyspnea within 1 day
rapid complete symptom reversal after bronchodilator
riskFactors: previous tuberculosis or chronic lung infection

name: Measles
treatment:
Main Treatment (Supportive + complication prevention)
Paracetamol 10–15mg/kg/dose PO every 6 hours PRN fever for 3–5 days
ORS frequent doses until hydrated
Adequate nutrition + fluids daily
Saline eye cleaning / lubricating drops PRN conjunctivitis

Essential Adjunct (All children/severe deficiency risk)
Vitamin A
<6 months: 50,000 IU PO day 1, repeat day 2
6–11 months: 100,000 IU PO day 1, repeat day 2
≥12 months: 200,000 IU PO day 1, repeat day 2
severe eye disease/malnutrition: repeat day 14

If Secondary Bacterial Infection Suspected
Amoxicillin-clavulanate for 5–7 days
OR
Ceftriaxone if severe pneumonia/unable oral intake

name: Epilepsy
treatment:
First line
Levetiracetam 500mg BID → titrate every 7 days up to 1500mg BID

Alternative (generalized seizures)
Valproate 10–15mg/kg/day → 20–30mg/kg/day maintenance

Alternative (focal seizures)
Carbamazepine 200mg BID → 800–1200mg/day

Adjunct (short-term control)
Clonazepam 0.5–2mg/day

Emergency status epilepticus
Lorazepam 4mg IV or diazepam 10mg IV, repeat once if needed

Maintenance duration: at least 2–5 years seizure-free before taper consideration
exclusionFeatures: documented acute metabolic cause only (e.g., hypoglycemia)

Cholecalciferol (vitamin d3) 50,000 IU orally once weekly for 6–8 weeks, then 1,000–2,000 IU daily maintenance

Severe Deficiency/Osteomalacia
Cholecalciferol 50,000 IU weekly for 8 weeks + calcium carbonate 500mg elemental calcium BID

If Obesity/Malabsorption/Poor Response
3,000–6,000 IU daily for 8 weeks, then maintenance

Monitoring
Recheck vitamin D + calcium after 8–12 weeks

Adjunct
Safe sunlight exposure
Improve nutrition (fortified dairy, eggs, fish)

symptoms.typicalSymptom: episodic postprandial pain after fatty meal

pathognomonicSymptoms: episodic postprandial pain after fatty meal

Diclofenac 75mg IM stat, then 50mg oral BID for 3 days
Hyoscine butylbromide 10mg oral TID PRN for 3 days adjunct
Ondansetron 4mg oral/IV every 8 hours PRN adjunct
Low-fat diet
Definitive best treatment: laparoscopic cholecystectomy

Ceftriaxone 1g daily + metronidazole 500mg every 8 hours for 5–7 days

Severe/resistant setting: Piperacillin-tazobactam 4.5g IV every 8 hours
Urgent surgery referral

Non-surgical candidate
Ursodeoxycholic acid 300mg BID for 6–12 months (selected cholesterol stones only)

name: CKD
treatment:
Core Renoprotective Therapy
Losartan 50mg daily, titrate to 100mg daily (monotherapy or combine with other antihypertensives)
Valsartan 80mg daily (alternative ARB)
Amlodipine 5mg daily, adjunct if BP uncontrolled, increase to 10mg daily

Fluid Overload
Furosemide 40mg orally daily, may increase to 40mg BID if edema

Diabetes + CKD
Empagliflozin 10mg daily (if diabetic CKD)

Anemia
Ferrous sulfate 325mg daily for 90 days if iron deficiency suspected

Acidosis
Sodium bicarbonate 650mg orally BID

CKD-MBD
Calcium carbonate 500mg with meals BID if phosphate high

Avoid/Safety
Avoid NSAIDs
Avoid dehydration
Adjust antibiotic/drug doses to kidney function

treatment:

Urgent appendectomy

Ceftriaxone 2g IV daily + metronidazole 500mg IV every 8h
Continue until surgery, usually <24h after surgery if uncomplicated

Complicated/perforated/abscess/severe sepsis
Piperacillin-tazobactam 4.5g IV every 6h
OR
Meropenem 1 g IV every 8h
Duration: 4–7 days after source control

Penicillin allergy
Ciprofloxacin 400mg IV every 12h + metronidazole 500mg IV every 8h

Supportive
IV fluids
Paracetamol
Ondansetron
NPO
Urgent surgical referral
exclusionFeatures: normal WBC + normal CRP after 24h symptoms

name: Gastroenteritis
treatment:
ORS: first-line, continuous replacement therapy
Zinc sulfate: 20mg daily (children >6 months), 10–14 days
Ondansetron: 0.15mg/kg every 8–12 hours PRN vomiting
NS: for moderate–severe dehydration or shock

Antibiotics (ONLY if dysentery/severe bacterial suspicion):
Azithromycin: 10mg/kg day 1, then 5mg/kg/day for 4 days
Ciprofloxacin (adults): 500mg BID for 3–5 days

Metronidazole: if giardiasis suspected (persistent foul watery diarrhea >7 days)
exclusionFeatures: persistent localized right lower quadrant pain without diarrhea
normal stool frequency with severe persistent abdominal pain

name: Rheumatoid Arthritis
treatment:
First-line standard (most patients)
Methotrexate 15mg once weekly, increase gradually to 20–25mg weekly if needed
Folic acid 1mg daily or 5mg weekly (not same day as methotrexate)
Trial: 8–12 weeks, then continue long-term if effective

Moderate/high disease activity (best combination)
Methotrexate 15–25mg weekly
Hydroxychloroquine 200mg BID
Sulfasalazine 500mg BID, increase to 1g BID

Flare/bridge adjunct
Prednisolone 5–10mg daily for 14–42 days then taper off

Pain adjunct
Naproxen 500mg BID PRN short course
OR
Celecoxib 200mg daily PRN

Monitoring
CBC, LFT, creatinine periodically
ophthalmology review if hydroxychloroquine long-term
exclusionFeatures: uric acid high with episodic podagra

Ceftriaxone 500mg IM once
Doxycycline 100mg BID for 14 days
Metronidazole 500mg BID for 14 days

Severe/Inpatient/Tubo-ovarian abscess
Ceftriaxone 1g IV daily + Doxycycline 100mg BID + Metronidazole 500mg orally/IV BID
Continue until clinically improved, then complete 14 days total

Cefixime 400mg BID + Doxycycline 100mg BID 14 days + Metronidazole 500mg BID 14 days

Treat sexual partne
Avoid intercourse until both treated
Pain control: paracetamol/ibuprofen
Urgent referral if pregnancy, sepsis, guarding, abscess, inability to tolerate oral drugs
exclusionFeatures: rapid diagnostic test pregnancy positive with pelvic pain
urine nitrite positive with isolated urinary symptoms

name: Hyperthyroidism
treatment:
First-line Monotherapy
Methimazole 10–30mg daily for 12–18 months
Preferred in most non-pregnant adults

Symptom Control (Adjunct with antithyroid drug)
Propranolol 20–40mg every 6–8 hours for 2–6 weeks

Pregnancy/Methimazole contraindication
Propylthiouracil (PTU) 100mg every 8 hours in 1st trimester
Then switch to methimazole when appropriate

Severe thyrotoxicosis/thyroid storm
PTU 200mg every 4 hours
Propranolol 40–80mg every 6 hours
Hydrocortisone 100mg IV every 8 hours
urgent hospital referral

Definitive Treatment
Radioactive iodine (if available)
surgery for large goiter, compression, relapse, or toxic nodular disease

Monitoring
TSH + free T4 every 4–6 weeks initially

name: Vaginitis
treatment:

Fluconazole 150mg orally single dose
OR
Clotrimazole vaginal tablet 500mg once
OR
Clotrimazole cream 2% nightly × 3 days

If bacterial vaginosis likely (odor + thin gray discharge)
Metronidazole 500mg orally BID × 7 days

Treat sexual partner simultaneously

If mixed vaginitis suspected
Metronidazole 500mg BID × 7 days + Clotrimazole vaginal therapy × 3–7 days

Adjunct
avoid scented soaps
optimize diabetes control
avoid unnecessary antibiotics

treatment:
Mild Disease (skin/joint)
Hydroxychloroquine 200mg once or twice daily long-term base therapy
Naproxen 500mg BID for 5–10 days adjunct if pain
Prednisolone 5–10mg daily short flare then taper

Moderate Disease
Hydroxychloroquine combined
Prednisolone 0.5mg/kg/day then taper
Methotrexate 10–20mg weekly if arthritis-predominant (with folate)

Severe Disease (nephritis/CNS/hematologic)
Methylprednisolone 500–1000mg IV daily × 3 days
then prednisolone taper
Mycophenolate mofetil 1g BID preferred induction

Supportive
calcium + vitamin D
BP/proteinuria control (ACEi/ARB if indicated)
eye monitoring with hydroxychloroquine
comprehensiveInfo: No additional information available.

name: Hyperlipidemia
treatment:
First-line Monotherapy
Atorvastatin 20mg nightly long-term
High ASCVD risk: atorvastatin 40–80mg nightly

Alternative Monotherapy
Rosuvastatin 10mg nightly long-term
High risk: rosuvastatin 20–40mg nightly

If triglycerides ≥500 mg/dL
Fenofibrate 145mg daily (monotherapy or adjunct carefully)

Non-drug Therapy
weight loss
exercise 150 min/week
stop smoking
reduce trans fat/saturated fat
reduce sugar/refined flour
increase fiber-rich foods

Monitoring
repeat lipid profile after 42–84 days, then every 180–365 days
exclusionFeatures: secondary acute illness with transient lipid change

name: Acute Pancreatitis
treatment:
Initial Core Management
RL IV 200–300mL/hour initially, adjust to BP, urine output, comorbidity
Nil per oral if severe vomiting/intense pain, then early oral feeding within 24 hours if tolerated
Oxygen if hypoxic

Pain Control
Paracetamol 1g IV/PO every 6–8 hours adjunct
Tramadol 50–100mg IV/PO every 6–8 hours if moderate pain

Antiemetic
Ondansetron 4–8mg IV/PO every 8 hours

Antibiotics
Not routine in uncomplicated pancreatitis
If cholangitis, infected necrosis, sepsis suspected:
Piperacillin-tazobactam 4.5g IV every 6 hours
OR
Ceftriaxone 2g IV daily + metronidazole 500mg IV every 8 hours
exclusionFeatures: no abdominal pain with normal abdominal exam
chronic unchanged dyspepsia without acute episode

name: Ankylosing Spondylitis
treatment:
First-line
Naproxen 500mg BID for 14–28 days
OR
Indomethacin 25–50mg 2–3 times daily for 14–28 days

Adjunct
omeprazole 20mg daily if NSAID gastritis risk
daily posture exercises
spinal mobility exercises
smoking cessation
exclusionFeatures: severe radicular pain with neurologic deficit

name: Ulcerative Colitis+
treatment:
Mild proctitis
Mesalazine suppository 1g rectal nightly for 4 to 8 weeks

Mild to moderate left-sided/extensive disease
Mesalazine oral 2.4 to 4.8g/day in divided doses for 8 weeks

Moderate flare not controlled on mesalazine
Prednisolone 40mg daily, taper over 6 to 8 weeks + continue mesalazine

Severe acute flare/hospitalized
Hydrocortisone IV 100mg every 6 hours for 3 to 5 days, then oral steroid taper if response

Remission maintenance
Mesalazine oral 1.6 to 2.4g/day long-term

Iron deficiency anemia
Ferrous sulfate providing 60 to 120mg elemental iron/day for 3 months 
Rehydration and electrolyte correction during flare

name: Crohn Disease+
treatment:
Mild to moderate disease
Prednisolone 40mg daily, taper over 8 weeks

Moderate to severe flare
Prednisolone 40 to 60mg daily then taper

Perianal disease/fistulizing symptoms
Metronidazole 400mg TID daily for 14 to 28 days

Supportive:
iron if Hb low
vitamin B12 if deficient
nutrition support
smoking cessation
exclusionFeatures: acute onset symptoms less than 7 days
rapid complete response to short antibiotic course
painless isolated rectal bleeding with no diarrhea

name: Fibromyalgia+
treatment:
Amitriptyline 10–25mg nightly for 8–12 weeks — first-line monotherapy

Duloxetine 30mg daily → increase to 60mg daily for 12 weeks — monotherapy if mood disorder prominent

Pregabalin 75mg nightly → 75mg BID → max 150mg BID for 8–12 weeks — adjunct or monotherapy

Paracetamol 500–1000mg every 6–8 hours PRN — supportive only
exclusionFeatures: inflammatory arthritis markers positive (e.g., rheumatoid factor positive with synovitis)

name: Tension Headache+
treatment:
Paracetamol 500mg to 1000mg orally every 6 to 8 hours as needed, max 3g/day, monotherapy

Ibuprofen 400mg every 8 hours as needed for 3 to 5 days, monotherapy

Naproxen 250mg to 500mg every 12 hours as needed for 3 to 5 days, monotherapy

Amitriptyline 10mg nightly, increase gradually to 25mg nightly if chronic/recurrent, duration 8 to 12 weeks, preventive monotherapy

Tizanidine 2mg nightly for 7 to 14 days if neck muscle spasm present, adjunct

Non-drug adjuncts: hydration, sleep correction, stress management, posture correction, regular meals

name: Cervical Spondylosis+
treatment:
Paracetamol 500mg to 1000mg every 6 to 8 hours for 7 to 14 days — monotherapy for mild pain

Naproxen 500mg BID for 7 to 14 days — first-line monotherapy

Ibuprofen 400mg TID for 5 to 10 days — alternative monotherapy

Tizanidine 2mg to 4mg at night for 7 to 14 days — adjunct combination for spasm

Pregabalin 75mg at night, may increase to 75mg BID for 14 to 42 days — adjunct for radicular neuropathic pain

Omeprazole 20mg daily during NSAID use — adjunct if GI risk

Physiotherapy, posture correction, cervical strengthening exercises for 4 to 12 weeks — core non-drug treatment

Short soft cervical collar for acute flare only, 3 to 7 days — temporary adjunct

Progressive neurologic deficit / cervical myelopathy: urgent surgical referral

name: Frozen Shoulder+
treatment:
pParacetamol 500 to 1000mg every 6 to 8 hours as needed for 14 to 30 days — monotherapy mild pain

Ibuprofen 400mg every 8 hours after food for 10 to 14 days — monotherapy if suitable

Naproxen 500mg every 12 hours for 10 to 14 days — alternative monotherapy

Intra-articular triamcinolone 20 to 40mg single injection — adjunct combination with exercise; may repeat after 6 to 12 weeks if needed

Omeprazole 20mg daily during NSAID use if GI risk — adjunct

Structured stretching and physiotherapy daily for 6 to 12 weeks — essential adjunct combination

Optimize diabetes and thyroid control if present — adjunct

name: Sciatica+

symptoms.typicalSymptom: pain worse with coughing or sneezing

treatment:
First-line monotherapy
Ibuprofen 400mg every 8 hours after food for 7–14 days

Alternative first-line monotherapy
Naproxen 500mg bid for 7–14 days

Adjunct combination
Paracetamol 500–1000mg every 8 hours as needed for pain

Neuropathic pain adjunct (persistent radicular pain)
Gabapentin 300mg nightly, titrate to 300mg tid for 14–28 days

Short severe flare option
Prednisolone 40mg daily for 5 days (selected patients only)

Non-drug adjunct
Remain active, walking, lumbar extension/flexibility exercises, posture correction

Urgent referral if: progressive weakness, foot drop, saddle anesthesia, bladder/bowel dysfunction, fever, trauma
cardinalSymptoms: pain worse with coughing or sneezing

name: Premature Ovarian Insufficiency+
treatment:
Estradiol 1–2mg daily + medroxyprogesterone 10mg daily for 12–14 days each month; first-line combination if uterus present; continue until age 50 years

Estrogen monotherapy if prior hysterectomy

Calcium carbonate 1000–1200mg daily + vitamin D3 800–1000 IU daily; adjunct long-term
bisphosphonate only if osteoporosis and HRT unsuitable

name: Polycystic Ovary Syndrome+
treatment:
Metformin 500mg daily with food, titrate to 1500–2000mg/day divided doses; first-line for insulin resistance/cycle irregularity; long-term monotherapy or combination

Ethinyl estradiol 30mcg + levonorgestrel 150mcg daily for 21/28-day cycle; first-line combination if pregnancy not desired

Letrozole 2.5mg daily for 5 days starting cycle day 3 to 5; first-line for infertility/ovulation induction

Spironolactone 50mg BID; adjunct for hirsutism/acne only with reliable contraception
topical benzoyl peroxide once daily; adjunct for acne

Lifestyle therapy: 5–10% weight loss + regular exercise; essential adjunct

name: Endometriosis+
treatment:
Ibuprofen 400mg every 8 hours with food during pain episodes; first-line monotherapy

Naproxen 500mg once then 250mg every 12 hours during symptoms; alternative first-line monotherapy

Ethinyl estradiol 30mcg + levonorgestrel 150mcg daily continuous or cyclic regimen for 6–12 months; first-line combination hormonal suppression

Norethisterone 5mg BID for 6 months; practical low-resource monotherapy option

Medroxyprogesterone acetate 10mg oral daily for 3–6 months; alternative monotherapy

Omeprazole 20mg oral daily during prolonged NSAID use; adjunct gastric protection

name: Non-Alcoholic Fatty Liver Disease+
treatment:
Weight reduction target 7 to 10% body weight + regular exercise; first-line essential therapy

Metformin 500mg daily, titrate to 1500–2000mg/day; adjunct especially if diabetes or insulin resistance

Pioglitazone 15–30mg daily for 6–12 months; option for suspected steatohepatitis, especially with diabetes

Vitamin E 400 IU BID for 6–12 months; option in selected non-diabetic adults

Atorvastatin 10–20mg daily; adjunct for dyslipidemia

Losartan 50mg daily if hypertension present; adjunct metabolic risk control

name: Lactose Intolerance+

symptoms.typicalSymptom: generalized abdominal pain after dairy intake
urgency to defecate after dairy intake
symptom onset within hours of dairy intake

pathognomonicSymptoms: generalized abdominal pain after dairy intake
treatment:
Lactose-restricted diet; first-line long-term therapy
Calcium carbonate 500mg BID if reduced dairy intake; adjunct
Vitamin D3 800 IU daily if deficiency risk; adjunct
Oral rehydration solution after diarrhea episodes; adjunct
cardinalSymptoms: urgency to defecate after dairy intake

name: Celiac Disease+
treatment:
Strict lifelong gluten-free diet; first-line and definitive therapy
Iron sulfate 325mg once to twice daily for 3–6 months; adjunct for iron deficiency anemia
Folic acid 5mg daily for 3 months; adjunct
Vitamin B12 1000mcg oral or IM monthly if deficient; adjunct
Vitamin D3 800–1000 IU oral daily + calcium carbonate 500–1000mg daily; adjunct for bone health
Nutritional rehabilitation with high-protein diet; supportive
exclusionFeatures: chronic bloody diarrhea without malabsorption pattern
persistent normal Hb and iron studies
isolated acute infectious diarrhea with fever

name: Pancreatic Insufficiency+
treatment:
Omeprazole 20mg oral daily; adjunct to enhance enzyme effectiveness
Fat-soluble vitamin supplementation (A, D, E, K) daily; long-term adjunct
High-calorie, high-protein diet; essential supportive therapy
exclusionFeatures: normal weight with normal appetite and persistent symptoms
normal vitamin A, D, E, K levels with chronic steatorrhea
IBS with normal stool consistency and no malabsorption

name: Pernicious Anemia+
treatment:
Cyanocobalamin IM — 1000mcg daily for 7 days, then weekly for 4 weeks, then monthly lifelong (monotherapy, first-line)
Hydroxocobalamin IM — 1000mcg every other day for 2 weeks, then every 2–3 months lifelong (alternative strong option)
Folic acid — 1mg daily for 3–4 months (adjunct, only after B12 started)
Iron supplementation — if concurrent deficiency (adjunct)
Blood transfusion — if severe symptomatic anemia (Hb <7g/dL) (supportive)
exclusionFeatures: vitamin B12 normal — strongly reduces likelihood
isolated iron deficiency signs without macrocytosis — unlikely

name: Weil's Disease+
treatment:
Severe disease (preferred first-line)
Ceftriaxone 2g IV once daily, 7 days

Mild to moderate disease
Doxycycline 100mg bid, 7 days

Adjunct combination (when needed)
IV fluids + electrolyte correction
dialysis for severe acute kidney injury
vasopressor support for refractory hypotension

name: Typhus+
treatment:
First-line monotherapy
Doxycycline 100mg bid for 7 days or until afebrile for 48 hours

Alternative
Azithromycin 500mg once daily for 5 days

Adjunct combination
Paracetamol 500–1000mg every 6–8 hours as needed
IV fluids if hypotension or dehydration
exclusionFeatures: rash absent after day 7 of untreated illness
riskFactors: residence in refugee camps or overcrowded shelters
poor hygiene with limited access to washing facilities

name: Hyperprolactinemia+
treatment:
First-line monotherapy
Cabergoline 0.25mg twice weekly; titrate every 4 weeks based on prolactin; duration Start 180 days, End 1825 days

Alternative
Bromocriptine 1.25mg at night for 7 days, then 2.5mg once or twice daily; duration Start 180 days, End 1825 days

Adjunct
Levothyroxine 25–100mcg daily if hypothyroidism present

Practical preference
cabergoline preferred due higher normalization rates, better tumor shrinkage, lower adverse effects, and better long-term adherence

name: Pheochromocytoma+
treatment:
First-line preoperative alpha blockade
Phenoxybenzamine 10mg bid; titrate every 2–3 days to 40–100mg/day — 10 to 14 days

Practical low-resource alternative
Prazosin 1mg bid; titrate to 6–15mg/day — 10 to 14 days

If persistent tachycardia after adequate alpha blockade
Propranolol 10–40mg every 8 hours

Volume expansion
increased salt intake plus oral fluids — 10 to 14 days

Hypertensive crisis
Phentolamine 1–5mg IV repeated as needed — acute adjunct

Definitive treatment
surgical adrenalectomy after adequate blockade
exclusionFeatures: persistent normotension during repeated spontaneous attacks

name: Trgeminal Neuralgia+

symptoms.typicalSymptom: weight loss secondary to fear of chewing

treatment:
First-line Monotherapy
Carbamazepine 100mg bid, increase every 3–7 days, usual effective dose 200–400mg bid, maximum 1200mg/day, duration Start 180 days, End 3650 days

Strong Alternative Monotherapy
Oxcarbazepine 150mg bid, titrate to 300–600mg bid, maximum 1800mg/day, duration Start 180 days, End 3650 days

Adjunct Combination (Refractory/Partial Response)
Baclofen 5mg tid, titrate to 10–20mg tid, duration Start 30 days, End 365 days, adjunct
Gabapentin 300mg nightly, titrate to 300–600mg tid, duration Start 30 days, End 365 days, adjunct

Low-resource Practical Priority
Carbamazepine remains the most evidence-based, available, and cost-effective first choice in Afghanistan.
exclusionFeatures: persistent continuous facial pain without paroxysms

name: Addison’s Disease+
treatment:
First-line maintenance therapy
Hydrocortisone 15–25mg/day orally in 2–3 divided doses; monotherapy in secondary adrenal insufficiency, lifelong

Primary adrenal insufficiency adjunct
Fludrocortisone 0.05–0.2mg orally OD; adjunct combination with hydrocortisone, lifelong

Low-resource alternative
Prednisolone 3–5mg OD; combination with fludrocortisone if primary disease, lifelong

Adrenal crisis
Hydrocortisone 100mg IV/IM stat, then 200mg over 24 hours (continuous infusion or divided doses), adjunct with isotonic saline ± dextrose, 1–3 days then oral conversion

name: Secondary Adrenal Failure+
treatment:
Stable cases
Hydrocortisone 15–25mg/day orally in 2–3 divided doses (10mg morning, 5mg afternoon, 5mg evening), lifelong or until HPA-axis recovery confirmed

If hydrocortisone unavailable
Prednisolone 3–5mg OD morning, lifelong or until recovery

Adrenal crisis — Adjunct combination
Hydrocortisone 100mg IV stat, then 50mg IV every 6 hours plus IV normal saline plus IV dextrose if hypoglycemia present, 24–72 hours then oral conversion

Important correction:
No mineralocorticoid replacement in secondary adrenal failure.
riskFactors: pituitary surgery or cranial radiotherapy history
